title: Zero-lock braking system
abstract: This invention relates to the zero-lock braking system that is used in order to ensure the safer and faster stop of motor vehicles and its distinctive characteristic is that it calculates optimum brake pressure or quantity by means of a black box (10) made up of fuzzy logic that will be applied to both hydraulic brake systems and also to brake-by-wire systems.
claims: Claims \. This invention relates to the zero-lock braking system and its distinctive characteristic is that it calculates optimum brake pressure or quantity by means of a black box (10) made up of fuzzy logic that will be applied to both hydraulic brake systems and also to brake-by-wire systems. 2. In Claim 1, reference is made to the zero-lock braking system that is characterized by having a black box (10) that ensures the vehicle's deceleration as a result of the input (caliper compression amount) (8 input) data for locking ratio (O) and pressure values applied onto caliper (b) (Figure-5). 3. In Claim 1, reference is made to the zero-lock braking system that is characterized by the calculation of the brake pressure*(l) difference between the point of 0.1% and the point Z (Figure-7). 4. This is zero-lock braking system that is characterized by the determination of an average ratio statistically between y and x and this ratio enables the calculation of pressure value Z (caliper compression amount) by subtracting y from the pressure value (caliper compression amount) corresponding the point 0.1% as the first step while braking (Figure-7). 5. In Claim 1, reference is made to the zero-lock braking system that is characterized by the inclusion of the point Z which is the optimum point for the system to operate. 6. In Claim 1, reference is made to the zero-lock braking system that is characterized by the updating and delivery by the control system of the pressure value (caliper compression ration) "z" obtained to the black box (10). 7. This is a zero-lock braking system conforming to Claim 6 and it is characterized by the continuous calculation and conveyance of the "z" value, which increases and decreases depending on the road conditions (such as surface friction value) that vary while braking, to the control system. 8. In Claim 1, reference is made to the zero-lock braking system that is characterized by having a black box (10) that has been trained as a result of the tests conducted under different ground conditions. 9. This is a zero-lock braking system conforming to Claim 8 and it is characterized by having a black box (10) with ÎÎ¶ as an output and with Ad, ÎÎ, Aa, AV, AFl, AF2, AF3, AF4 as inputs. 10. This is a zero-lock braking system conforming to Claim 8 or Claim 9 and it is characterized by having an algorithm that calculates the ÎÎ¶ value received from the black box (10). 11. In Claim 1, reference is made to the zero-lock braking system that is characterized by having the black box (10) inputs of Aa, ÎÎ½, ÎÎ, AFl, AF2 for the motorbike front wheel. 12. This is a zero-lock braking system conforming to Claim 11 and it is characterized by having a brake-by-wire system that contains a decline sensor, wheel speed sensor, acceleration sensor, and one torque sensor for each one of both wheels. 13. In Claim 1, reference is made to the zero-lock braking system that is characterized by having the black box (10) inputs of Aa, ÎÎ½, ÎÎ, AFl, AF2, AF3, and AF4 for the passenger car front wheel. 14. This is a zero-lock braking system conforming to Claim 13 and it is characterized by having a decline sensor, wheel speed sensor, acceleration sensor, and one torque sensor for each one of the for the four wheels, besides a hydraulic system that contains a brake fluid pressure sensor. 15. In Claim 1, reference is made to the zero-lock braking system that is characterized for aircrafts by having the black box (10) inputs of Aa, ÎÎ½, AFl, AF2, AF3, AF4, AF5, and AF6 for each wheel should the aircraft has 2 front, 2 right, and 2 left landing gears. 16. This is a zero-lock braking system conforming to Claim 15 and it is characterized by the use of a wheel speed sensor, acceleration sensor, and one torque sensor for each wheel, a brake fluid pressure sensor (in hydraulic systems). 17. In Claim 1, reference is made to the zero-lock braking system that is characterized by having black boxes (10) in a number that is equal to the number of wheels.
